assertion_results.text: A base exception class (AppError or similar) is defined with attributes: code, message, status_code, category, and support for __cause__ chaining
assertion_results.evidence: AppError base class in exceptions.py with code, message, status_code, category attributes. Uses 'raise ... from e' pattern for __cause__ chaining throughout.

assertion_results.text: At least 4 domain-specific exception subclasses exist (e.g., NotFoundError, ValidationError, ServiceUnavailableError, PaymentError)
assertion_results.evidence: Found 6 subclasses: NotFoundError, ValidationError, ServiceUnavailableError, PaymentError, ConflictError, AuthenticationError. Each with appropriate default codes and status codes.

assertion_results.text: Exception wrapping uses 'raise NewError(...) from original_error' syntax to preserve the causal chain
assertion_results.evidence: All service functions use 'raise X from e' syntax consistently. Example: 'raise ServiceUnavailableError("Database connection failed", code="ERR_DB_CONNECT") from e'

assertion_results.text: No bare 'except:' or 'except Exception:' clauses that swallow errors without re-raising or wrapping
assertion_results.evidence: All except clauses catch specific types (SQLAlchemyError, IntegrityError, httpx.HTTPStatusError, httpx.RequestError) and re-raise as wrapped AppError subclasses. No swallowed exceptions.

assertion_results.text: Structured logging outputs JSON-formatted log entries with at minimum: code, message, correlation_id, and timestamp
assertion_results.evidence: Uses python-json-logger with custom JsonFormatter. Log entries include: code, message, correlation_id, timestamp, category, exc_info, service_name.

assertion_results.text: Database errors (SQLAlchemy exceptions) are caught and wrapped in domain-specific errors with context
assertion_results.evidence: SQLAlchemyError caught in all DB functions. IntegrityError caught separately for constraint violations. Wrapped with context: 'raise ServiceUnavailableError("Failed to create order", code="ERR_DB_CREATE_ORDER", details={"table": "orders"}) from e'

assertion_results.text: External API errors (httpx/HTTP errors) are caught and wrapped with the upstream service name and response details
assertion_results.evidence: httpx.HTTPStatusError caught with upstream details: 'raise ServiceUnavailableError("Inventory sync failed", code="ERR_UPSTREAM_INVENTORY", details={"service": "inventory-api", "status": e.response.status_code, "body": e.response.text[:200]}) from e'

assertion_results.text: Each error includes a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_ORDER_NOT_FOUND) not just a status number
assertion_results.evidence: All errors use string codes: ERR_ORDER_NOT_FOUND, ERR_VALIDATION_ORDER, ERR_DB_CREATE_ORDER, ERR_PAYMENT_FAILED, ERR_UPSTREAM_INVENTORY, ERR_PAYMENT_GATEWAY, ERR_DB_CONNECT
